lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me? Are you there?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Oh! Sorry, sailor, I didn't see you there. My name is Gwyna. It's nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice to meet you too, Gwyna. My name is [player name], Captain of the [boat name].

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: What a delightful name for a boat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. If you don't mind me asking, what were you doing earlier?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: I was singing for those who perished here. What surrounds you are the remains of hundreds of elven vessels which were destroyed during the Siege of Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Their remains are scattered across the waters here, creating what sailors refer to as the crystal-flecked waters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I didn't realise that's what the crystals were.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: You've no need to feel sad here, adventurer. This is where we honour the dead by singing songs to remember them.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: That's the main objective of this Barracuda Trial, the Gwenith Glide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so this is a Barracuda Trial. What do I do if I want to get involved?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Well, unfortunately I don't think you have the Sailing experience needed to attempt this trial. I'd suggest sailing elsewhere until you are ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice. I'll bear that in mind.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: The objective of this trial is to imbue large crystals dedicated to the different elven clans. You do that by sailing through portals linked to them. To get started, head out into the course in search of the portals.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: While you're out there, keep an eye out for any lost supplies. You'll need to collect all of them to complete the trial.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: The portals you're looking for are found next to the large crystals. Once you find one, just sail right through it. As you do, the portal will teleport you elsewhere in the crystal-flecked waters.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: After being teleported, you'll need to find a second portal to return to the trial area near me, ideally corresponding to the one you went through. The portal you choose to return through determines which crystal

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: is imbued.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: As you sail around, you'll find crystal motes all across the crystal-flecked waters. If your boat is kitted out with an activated wind catcher, you'll be able to turn their energy into wind motes, allowing you to go even

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: faster.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: In order to complete the trial, a specific number of crystals will need to be imbued. Each one is imbued in the same way - by traversing the portal linked to it.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: The number of crystals that need imbuing will depend on the rank you're going for. The higher the rank, the more you'll need to imbue.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: This course will truly test your capabilities as a sailor. You'll find yourself alternating between navigating narrow spaces and vast wide open ones with the required speed and grace.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: While navigating the more narrow spaces, you should be wary of resonance crystals. These old ship defence systems can crystallise your helm, damaging those holding it.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: If your helm does get crystallised, you should be able to break the crystals with three swift strikes.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: When using the portals, you'll find that each crystal near me has a pair of them near it. Both will send you to the same location.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: However, upon using a return portal, your arrival location will depend on which portal you first picked. Choosing which portals to use and planning ahead of time are critical for overcoming this trial.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: If you can imbue all of the crystals and collect all the lost supplies in the time given, you'll be awarded a Barracuda rank. Each trial has three ranks: Swordfish, Shark or Marlin. Each rank is harder than the last.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: For this trial you'll be asked to imbue additional crystals and collect even more lost supplies as the rank increases.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: You can't attempt the next rank until you beat the one before it. You can also try unranked if you'd like to warm up beforehand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Do you have any tips or tricks for me?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Of course, that's why I'm here.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: To start, traversing the portals will reward your boat with an incredible burst of speed, so use that to your advantage.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Additionally, be aware that the portals will both displace your boat and change which direction it is headed for.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Aligning yourself properly before going through is not required, but you should try to anticipate where you'll end up and react quickly.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Next, avoid the resonance crystals in the area. They will target the helm of your boat. If you do get hit by them, make sure to break the crystals rapidly. Doing so will grant your boat a small speed boost.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Another thing to look out for are the crystal motes. If you have a wind catcher or gale catcher activated on your boat, they will be converted into wind motes and charge it up for you.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: If you don't have a wind catcher, I'd suggest attempting the Jubbly Jive trial over in Backwater.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Finally, keep your sails trimmed and try not to get lost out there. The portals can be disorientating at first, but I'm sure you'll learn the layout of the course over time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice. I'll bear that in mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the Gwenith Glide?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Certainly. The large crystals you see around us are actually remnants of large ships that were destroyed during the Siege of Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Elven ships are not constructed from wood and metal, but are instead sung and woven into life over many moons from extraordinarily rare crystal seeds.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: The process is incredibly intimate and time consuming. Each ship created represents not just our goddess, but also the individual who brought it into existence.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Like all Barracuda Trials, the Gwenith Glide exists to push your sailing skills to the limit. However, it also acts as an opportunity to honour not just those that died here, but also the ships themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is it that I need to do within the trial?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: The objective of this trial is to imbue large crystals dedicated to the different elven clans. You do that by sailing through portals linked to them. To get started, head out into the course in search of the portals.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: My name is Gwyna of the Hefin Clan. I'm a trial master for the Barracudas and I provide guidance and counsel to those who attempt this trial, the Gwenith Glide.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: It is my job to see that those who died here are honoured and that the sanctity of the course is upheld.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do those goals ever conflict? I'm not sure how the sanctity of the course is upheld as sailors race around the place.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: I appreciate your concern, but you need not worry. Respect for the dead can be upheld in many ways. All who take on the trial have my blessing to proceed as they wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are the Barracudas?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: The organisation is a collection of like-minded individuals interested in furthering their own expertise and knowledge of sailing.

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: We're open to all with the spirit and patience to learn this delicate and precise skill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I become a Barracuda?

lines.speaker: Gwyna
lines.text: Of course you can, but you'll have to prove yourself first. There are other trial masters like me across the seas. You'll need to overcome our trials to earn your place among us.

sections.title: When spoken to from land, or from a boat without having completed Regicide

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me? Are you there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guess she can't hear me from over here.
